page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-48-lssd is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 186.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource density of approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per vCPU core, aligning with standard general-purpose computing profiles.

The instance does not include integrated graphics processing units, with a GPU count of zero. Local storage is specified as none in the hardware configuration. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external networking configurations for public internet access.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio position this instance for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the overhead of specialized hardware. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose web serving, application servers, and distributed systems that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-48-lssd is a general-purpose server instance from the c4d family. It is configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture and 186.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 3.88 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not feature bundled local storage or graphics processing units, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for workloads requiring stable, dedicated compute resources, the server provides a balanced allocation of memory and processing power. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and middle-tier enterprise workloads that do not rely on local SSDs or hardware acceleration. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention.
